summary:
Children with tetralogy of fallot typically use the squatting position during cyanotic episodes (“tet spells”) to alleviate symptoms. This position increases systemic vascular resistance, reducing right-to-left shunting across the ventricular septal defect (VSD). It also enhances venous return and stabilizes intrathoracic pressures, supporting improved cerebral perfusion and symptom relief. The evidence supports this as a well-documented physiological adaptation in clinical practice. Thus, the correct answer is C — Squatting.

Claim: Children with tetralogy of fallot use the squatting position during cyanotic spells to relieve symptoms.  
Evidence: - Increases systemic vascular resistance, reducing right-to-left shunt across the ventricular septal defect. - Enhances venous return and stabilizes intrathoracic pressures, improving cerebral perfusion.  
Answer: C
